groups.title: African Clawed Frog (Xenopus Laevis)
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Description: • They have no tongue or external ears. • Their skin is $31$. • The mucus on their skin is for $32$. • Male frogs are $33$ the size of female frogs. Habitats: • They prefer $34$ water. • They live in oases or glacier-covered lakes. • They live in a condition with a high level of $35$. • In the condition of drought, they can survive in $36$ for a long period of time. Diet: • They use the sense of $37$ to capture food. • They use their claws to tear apart ample food. • They use their front feet and a unique $38$ system when feeding. Other information: • People in some states of the USA regard them as $39$. • The mucus on their skin has $40$ properties.

questions.title: The mucus on their skin is for 【   】.
Male frogs are 【   】 the size of female frogs.
They live in a condition with a high level of 【   】.
In the condition of drought, they can survive in 【   】 for a long period of time.
They use the sense of 【   】 to capture food.
They use their front feet and a unique 【   】 system when feeding.

transcript.en: Today I'd like to look at another type of amphibian - the African clawed frog, whose Latin name is Xenopus laevis.
Its name is derived from the three short claws on each hind foot, which it uses to tear apart its food.
The Latin word Xenopus means 'strange foot' and laevis means 'smooth'.
I'll start with a general description.
The African clawed frog is a species of frog that can be commonly found in the eastern and southern parts of Africa.
They have a flattened head and body but no tongue or external ears.
As the Latin name suggests, African clawed frogs have smooth skin, unlike the toad, whose skin always has a rough texture.
The body of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which can give their sensitive skin protection, thus playing an indispensable role in their survival.
Male and female African clawed frogs can be easily distinguished through several characteristics, and the major one is their size.
Generally, male frogs are about half as big as females.
Specifically, female frogs are usually 4.5 inches long, weighing around 7 ounces, while males are generally 2.5 inches long and 2 ounces heavy.
Then let's turn to their habitats.
African clawed frogs are entirely aquatic frogs, so they live in water all their life, whether they are tadpoles or adult frogs.
Although they can live in a wide range of freshwater environments, they particularly like still water instead of fast-flowing streams.
During the summer, African clawed frogs are mainly found in warm rivers, and during the rainy season, they tend to move into flooded forests.
In the wild, African clawed frogs are highly adaptable to changes in the environment around them, whether in oases or glacier-covered lakes.
Besides, they can even survive in water with high salt levels.
It is because African clawed frogs have a unique physiological mechanism in their body that could automatically regulate the balance of water inside and outside their body.
In dry weather, African clawed frogs always burrow themselves into mud, where they can survive for more than a year.
They are incredibly hardy and long-lived, with an average lifespan of five to fifteen years.
Some are even recorded to have lived for twenty to twenty-five years.
However, African clawed frogs are susceptible to the pollution of the water even though they are not listed as endangered species.
Now, let's talk about the diet of African clawed frogs.
They are carnivores that like hunting insects, spiders, worms, fish etc.
African clawed frogs depend on the sense of smell to detect their prey.
They first use the claws on their hind feet to tear pieces of ample food, then the front feet, which are not webbed, play an important role in feeding.
African clawed frogs use their front feet to push food into their mouths, and meanwhile, a distinctive pump system is used to draw or suck food in their mouths.
So we can see African clawed frogs are voracious predators and can quickly adapt to many habitats.
Therefore, in some parts of the world, African clawed frogs act like invasive species which will negatively affect other species in the freshwater ecosystems.
The evidence shows that they have devastated the populations of native frogs and other creatures by eating their young.

As a result, African clawed frogs are illegal to own, transport, or sell without any permit in some states of America like California, Washington, Arizona etc., where African clawed frogs have been seen as a type of pest.
However, it's legal to own African clawed frogs in Canada.
But not many people know that African clawed frogs are also used in medicine.
As we mentioned before, the skin of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which has properties of antibiotic.
This chemical can help kill bacteria and cure the infection and is widely used by both the medical profession and the general public.
So we shouldn't ignore the medical value of African clawed frogs.

31.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 skin；提示词为 is，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：开篇在稍作背景介绍后，小标题 description 和上层信息 no tongue or external ears 先后出现，提示定位到该题。接着原文提到 African clawed frogs have smooth skin，其中 smooth 搭配 skin 出现，符合题干要求，可判断其为正确答案。注意，之后原文提到 unlike the toad, whose skin always has a rough texture，根据否定词 unlike，可排除 rough（toad 的皮肤特点）。

32.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 mucus；提示词为 for，此处应填名词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，原文提到 which can give their sensitive skin protection，其中 which 指代前面的 mucus，可判断 protection 为正确答案。本题难度不大。

33.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 male frogs、female frogs；提示词为 are、size，此处应填表示倍数的词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，根据题干要求抓取表示倍数的词。原文提到 male frogs are about half as big as females，其中 big 对应题干的 size，可判断 half 为正确答案。

34.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 habitats；提示词为 prefer、water，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：小标题 habitats 原词重现后，可以听到题干的 water 出现（they live in water all their life），但其中并无修饰 water 的词。继续往下听，听到 they particularly like still water，其中 particularly like 对应题干的 prefer，still 修饰 water，可判断其为正确答案。注意，之后听到 instead of fast-flowing streams，根据否定词组 instead of 可判断 fast-flowing 为干扰信息。

35.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 oases、glacier-covered lakes；提示词为 live、high level of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题与上一题之间间隔时间较长，考生需耐心听下去。当听到上层信息 oases 和 glacier-covered lakes 原词重现时，可以定位到该题，准备抓取答案词。之后听到 “Besides, they can even survive in water with high salt levels.”，其中 survive in 对应题干的 live in；water 对应 condition，可判断 salt 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

36.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 drought；提示词为 survive in、a long period of time，此处应填名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，可以听到 in dry weather，其中 dry 对应题干的 drought，提示定位到该题。接着听到 “… African clawed frogs always burrow themselves into mud, where they can survive for more than a year”，其中 more than a year 对应题干的 a long period of time，可判断 mud 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

37.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 diet；提示词为 sense of、capture food，此处应填名词，表示某种感觉官能。
题目解析：听力录音稍作停顿后，小标题 diet 原词重现，提示定位到该题。根据题干要求直接抓取感官类名词，原文提到 “African clawed frogs depend on the sense of smell to detect their prey.”，其中 detect their prey 对应题干的 capture food；sense of smell 与题干结构一致，可判断 smell 为正确答案。

38.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 front feet；提示词为 unique、system、feeding，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：定位词 front feet 原词重现后，答案句并没有随后出现，而是在解释 front feet 的作用。接着听到 meanwhile 一词，考生可通过这个并列信号词辅助定位。随后听到 a distinctive pump system is used to draw or suck food in their mouths，其中 distinctive 对应题干的 unique，draw or suck food in their mouths 对应 feeding，可判断 pump 前后信息与题干相符，为正确答案。本题难度较大。

39.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 states of the USA；提示词为 regard as，此处应填名词或形容词。
题目解析：本题与上一题之间间隔时间较长，考生需耐心听下去。当听到 “… states of America like California, Washington, Arizona etc. ...” 时，可以定位到该题。随后原文提到 “… where African clawed frogs have been seen as a type of pest,”，其中 see as 对应题干的 regard as，可判断 pest 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

40.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 mucus、skin；提示词为 has、properties，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，原文提到 “… the skin of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which has properties of antibiotic”，其中 antibiotic 搭配 properties，符合题干要求，为正确答案。本题所考查的词汇难度较大。
